title: Extending the Uniface Server Docker Image
breadcrumbs: Extending the Uniface Server Docker Image
text: Extending the Uniface Server Docker Image You can extend the base Uniface Server Docker image as required. For example, to add specific database drivers that need extra Linux packages added to the image, or to change the environment before the Uniface Router is started, such as updating the library loading path. You may also want to package your application service as a new base image to simplify deployment. To extend the image, you need to build a new image based on the Uniface Server Docker image. In the examples that follow, Dockerfile is used. A Dockerfile starts with the base image followed by a list of commands to tailor the image. In many cases, these are calls to standard Linux commands to install additional packages, copy files, and tailor the configuration. Example 1: Add Oracle Client Libraries used by the Uniface Driver This Dockerfile creates an image with an Oracle instant client added, and updates the library loading path: # Start with the base Uniface Server image FROM rocketsoftware/uniface:latest USER root # Install Oracle 18 instant client packages for Uniface ORA 7.3 driver RUN microdnf install -y --nodocs libaio && / rpm -ivh https://download.oracle.com/otn_software/linux/instantclient/185000/oracle-instantclient18.5-basic-18.5.0.0.0-3.x86_64.rpm && / ln -s /usr/lib64/libnsl.so.2 /usr/lib64/libnsl.so.1 && / microdnf clean all && rm -rf /var/cache/* # Add Oracle client libs and add updated LD_LIBRARY_PATH to the initialization script unifacerc RUN echo 'export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib/oracle/18.5/client64/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH' >> /home/uniface/unifacerc USER uniface Copy Example 2: Add Application Context Files This Dockerfile copies the local application context into the image: # Start with the base Uniface Server image FROM rocketsoftware/uniface USER root # Copy the local application context into the image context COPY bookings /home/uniface/application/ USER uniface Copy Build a Custom Image To build your custom Uniface image once the Dockerfile is complete, use the docker build command: #build an image named custom_db_unifaceimage docker build . -t custom_db_unifaceimage #if your docker file is outside the build context use the -f switch. docker build . -t finance_microservice -f /path_to_dockerfiles/dockerfile Copy Once the image is built, run the container as normal, specifying your new image name instead of the base Uniface Server Docker image: #for the custom driver example the application context volume mount is still required docker run --rm -p 13005:13001 -v /app/payroll:/home/uniface/application/ -e "AUTH_PASSWORD=MY5uP3r$3cr3T" -it custom_db_unifaceimage #for the packaged application example the application context volume mount is not required docker run --rm -p 13005:13001 -e "AUTH_PASSWORD=MY5uP3r$3cr3T" -it finance_microservice Copy Was this topic helpful?
